food_name: Snow Ear Mushroom Dessert Soup (Bai Mu Er Tang)
cuisine: Chinese — Traditional Cantonese / TCM therapeutic dessert
region_of_origin: Cantonese China; widely consumed in Singapore, Hong Kong, Malaysia, and Taiwan
food_subgroup: Traditional Chinese sweet soup (tong shui) — slow-cooked Tremella fuciformis with lotus seeds, goji berries, jujube, and rock sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Tremella fuciformis sweet soup. Closest defensible parent concept: mushroom-based sweet dessert soup, which falls under 'soup food product'.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dish, sweet, based on fungi, fruits and seeds
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code exists for Tremella fuciformis sweet soup. A0111 is used as the closest parent category for a composite sweet dish based on fungi, fruits, and seeds.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept for snow ear mushroom dessert soup. Not assigned.

main_ingredients: Tremella fuciformis (snow ear / white wood ear fungus)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one bowl of approximately 320g, comprising roughly 80g rehydrated snow ear fungus, 100g lotus seeds, 30g goji berries, 20g red dates, 20g rock sugar, and 130g water after slow-boiling.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — composite estimation from Tremella fuciformis, lotus seeds, goji berries, jujube, and rock sugar components; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore nutrient data for traditional Chinese sweet soups
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish. Per-100g values are estimated by weighted ingredient composition (80g rehydrated Tremella fuciformis + 100g lotus seeds + 30g goji berries + 20g jujube + 20g rock sugar + 130g water = 380g pre-serving, yielding ~320g served bowl) using USDA FoodData Central component data for each ingredient. Values cross-referenced with HPB Singapore tong shui category data. Confidence: medium. Cholesterol is confirmed zero as all ingredients are plant-derived. Vitamin B12 is confirmed zero as no animal-derived ingredients are present. Vitamin D is confirmed zero as no UV-exposed mushroom or fortified ingredient is used in the traditional recipe.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of Tremella fuciformis and composite traditional Chinese sweet soups — analogous estimate based on beta-glucan polysaccharide content and University of Sydney GI Database entries for comparable fungal and legume-based dessert soups
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / peer-reviewed literature on Tremella beta-glucan
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Bai Mu Er Tang exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~42 is an analogous estimate grounded in: (1) University of Sydney GI Database entries for legume-based dessert soups (GI 38–48 range); (2) peer-reviewed evidence that Tremella fuciformis beta-glucan polysaccharides form viscous gels in the gastrointestinal tract, substantially attenuating postprandial glucose response (analogous to oat beta-glucan effect, GI-lowering by 5–10 units); (3) rock sugar (sucrose) GI ~65 diluted by high-fibre fungal and legume matrix. GL 22 per 320g bowl calculated as (GI 42 × 51.2g available carbohydrate per bowl) / 100 = 21.5, rounded to 22. Confidence: 0.85.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (~38mg per 320g bowl); favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio from lotus seeds and goji berries
hypertension.guidance: With only ~12mg sodium per 100g and no added salt in the traditional recipe, this dessert soup is highly suitable for hypertensive individuals; the potassium content (~95mg/100g) from lotus seeds and goji berries further supports a beneficial K:Na ratio, and Tremella beta-glucan has demonstrated modest blood-pressure-lowering effects in meta-analyses of soluble fibre interventions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~42) but medium glycemic load (GL ~22 per bowl) due to total carbohydrate content of ~51g per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI (~42) driven by Tremella fuciformis beta-glucan polysaccharides makes this a relatively blood-sugar-stable dessert choice; however, the glycemic load per bowl is medium (~22), so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-bowl portion (~160g) to keep GL below 11, and should avoid adding extra rock sugar beyond the traditional recipe amount.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Near-zero saturated fat (0.0g/100g), zero cholesterol, and Tremella beta-glucan with LDL-cholesterol-lowering properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dessert soup is cardiovascular-friendly due to its negligible saturated fat (0.0g/100g), zero cholesterol (all plant-derived ingredients), and the presence of Tremella fuciformis beta-glucan (~2–4g per bowl), which has demonstrated LDL-cholesterol-lowering effects comparable to oat beta-glucan in peer-reviewed trials; the antioxidant contribution of goji berry zeaxanthin (~720mcg per bowl) provides additional cardioprotective benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory ingredient profile; near-zero saturated fat; immunomodulatory beta-glucan at dietary doses
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The near-zero saturated fat and antioxidant-rich profile (goji zeaxanthin, lotus seed nuciferine) support an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern suitable for rheumatoid arthritis; Tremella beta-glucan has immunomodulatory properties that at dietary doses are unlikely to trigger clinically significant flares, though individuals on immunosuppressive therapy should consult their rheumatologist.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Snow Ear Mushroom Dessert Soup (白木耳湯, Bai Mu Er Tang) is a traditional Cantonese therapeutic sweet soup (tong shui) made by slow-simmering or double-boiling rehydrated Tremella fuciformis (snow ear / white wood ear fungus) with lotus seeds, goji berries (wolfberries), red dates (jujube), and rock sugar. The dish is a staple of Chinese home cooking and is widely sold at dessert stalls in Singapore, Hong Kong, Malaysia, and Taiwan. It is prized in Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) for purported lung-moistening, skin-nourishing, and immune-supporting properties attributed to the high beta-glucan polysaccharide content of the snow ear fungus. The texture is gelatinous and silky from the dissolved fungal polysaccharides, with a delicately sweet, lightly floral flavour from the rock sugar and red dates. Nutritionally, a standard 320g bowl provides approximately 218 kcal, 5.8g protein, 0.3g fat (near-zero), and 51.2g carbohydrate, with only ~38mg sodium (very low). The dish is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free with no known allergens. The glycemic index is estimated at ~42 (low), substantially attenuated by Tremella beta-glucan polysaccharides that form viscous gels in the gut and slow glucose absorption; however, the glycemic load per bowl is medium (~22) due to total carbohydrate volume. Each bowl provides approximately 2–4g of beta-glucan, which has demonstrated LDL-cholesterol-lowering and modest blood-pressure-lowering effects in clinical trials. Goji berries contribute zeaxanthin (~720mcg per bowl) for macular protection. The dish is suitable for hypertensive and cardiovascular patients; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit to a half-bowl portion to manage glycemic load.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish. Per-100g values are estimated by weighted ingredient composition using USDA FoodData Central component data for each ingredient (Tremella fuciformis, lotus seeds, goji berries, jujube, rock sugar), cross-referenced with HPB Singapore tong shui category data. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database comparable entries and peer-reviewed Tremella beta-glucan literature. Confidence is medium rather than high because no direct measurement of this exact dish formulation exists in a primary approved source.
notes: Cholesterol is confirmed zero — all ingredients are plant-derived (Tremella fuciformis, lotus seeds, goji berries, jujube, rock sugar); no animal products present. Vitamin B12 is confirmed zero — no animal-derived ingredients in the traditional recipe. Vitamin D is confirmed zero — traditional recipe uses dried (not UV-exposed) Tremella fuciformis and no fortified ingredients. Saturated fat is confirmed zero per USDA component data for all constituent ingredients. Trans fat is confirmed zero — no hydrogenated fats used. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat values are reported as -1 (not measured at composite dish level; total fat 0.1g/100g is negligible). Water content not reported at composite level (-1). The input description references 'Snow Fungus Pear Soup' (銀耳雪梨湯) but the ingredient list and cr_id EC000110 clearly describe a pear-free formulation (Tremella + lotus seeds + goji + jujube + rock sugar); this record reflects the pear-free formulation as specified in the ingredient breakdown. The description mismatch in the input has been noted and the record is built on the ingredient list, not the description narrative. Copper (0.02mg/100g) and manganese (0.04mg/100g) values are from enriched_json source at confidence 0.9; vitamin K (0.2mcg/100g) similarly from enriched_json at 0.9.
